manifest_files.content:
# iOS conventions

- Prefer SwiftUI for product UI.
- Keep generated client code behind thin app-owned adapters.

tasks.description: Add gioe/ios-libs as a Swift Package dependency and make SharedKit plus APIClient importable from the app target.
criteria: gioe/ios-libs is declared as a Swift Package dependency
SharedKit and APIClient compile in the app target
migration_hints: Remove duplicate local design-token definitions after SharedKit is wired

modules.name: SharedKit design system
modules.description: Shared SwiftUI design tokens, typography, spacing, and reusable controls.

pillars.name: Native feel
pillars.claim: Use platform SwiftUI conventions before custom visual systems.

glossary.definition: The ios-libs Swift package module that owns shared design tokens and base SwiftUI components.

context_atoms.content: Use SharedKit as the source of truth for app-wide iOS design primitives.

tasks.description: Import SharedKit and replace ad-hoc colors, spacing, and typography with shared design tokens.
criteria: SharedKit is imported from at least one app-owned SwiftUI view
A first screen uses shared colors, spacing, or typography tokens

verification_hints: Run swift build or the Xcode app build after adding the package.
Open the first SwiftUI screen and confirm SharedKit-backed styling renders.

modules.name: APIClient wiring
modules.description: Shared HTTP client setup and OpenAPI-oriented request boundaries.

context_atoms.content: The app will keep APIClient behind an app-owned factory so generated endpoint code can change independently.

tasks.summary: Connect APIClient to the first backend endpoint
tasks.description: Create an app-owned APIClient factory and use it from the first workflow that needs network data.
criteria: The app has one app-owned APIClient factory
The first networked workflow uses APIClient rather than ad-hoc URLSession calls
Failure and loading states are covered in the first workflow

verification_hints: Run the API client's unit tests or a mocked endpoint test.
Confirm the first workflow handles non-200 responses.

modules.name: Navigation shell
modules.description: A small SwiftUI app shell for tabs, stacks, and first workflow routing.

context_atoms.content: Start with a typed SwiftUI route enum so first-workflow navigation stays explicit.

tasks.summary: Build the first SwiftUI navigation shell
tasks.description: Create the app's root navigation structure and connect it to the first user workflow.
criteria: The root view owns a typed route model
The first workflow can navigate to a detail or completion screen
Navigation state is covered by at least one lightweight test or preview

verification_hints: Run the app in Simulator and exercise the first workflow navigation.

modules.name: Persistence boundary
modules.description: A minimal persistence abstraction for local drafts, cache state, or offline-first workflow data.

context_atoms.content: Local persistence should be introduced behind a protocol before concrete storage choices spread through views.

tasks.summary: Add local persistence for first workflow state
tasks.description: Choose a local storage mechanism and hide it behind the app-owned persistence boundary.
criteria: First workflow state survives app restart when persistence is required
Views depend on an app-owned persistence protocol rather than a concrete storage API

verification_hints: Run a persistence round-trip unit test.
Restart the app and confirm the first workflow restores expected state.

modules.name: Observability hooks
modules.description: Shared logging and event boundaries for launch, network, and first-workflow diagnostics.

context_atoms.content: Emit first-workflow diagnostic events through an app-owned observability boundary.

tasks.summary: Add observability for first workflow failures
tasks.description: Record structured diagnostics for launch, API failure, and first workflow completion paths.
criteria: The first workflow emits a success or completion event
Network and persistence failures include enough context for debugging

verification_hints: Trigger a mocked workflow failure and confirm the diagnostic event is emitted.

modules.name: Test scaffolding
modules.description: Starter test targets, previews, and fixtures for first-workflow confidence.

optional_files.content:
# First workflow tests

Start with model, routing, persistence, and API-client boundary tests before UI automation.

context_atoms.content: Prefer fast unit tests for first-workflow state before adding slower simulator UI tests.

tasks.description: Create starter tests for route state, API-client boundaries, persistence, and view model behavior.
criteria: First workflow has a fast test for success state
First workflow has a fast test for failure or empty state
The configured test command runs those tests

verification_hints: Run the configured test command before marking the first workflow task done.
